title: file backend: non-Unix put() creates file with unrestricted permissions
description: secretx-file/src/lib.rs write_secret_file() on non-Unix uses std::fs::write() which creates the file with default permissions (typically world-readable on Windows). The Unix path explicitly sets 0600. Trigger: Windows or non-Unix system calls file put() → secret file created readable by all users on the system. Should set restrictive ACLs on Windows or document the platform limitation explicitly.
